title: America Before: The Key to Earth's Lost Civilization

description.en: Graham Hancock's 2019 work focuses the catastrophist hypothesis on the Americas — arguing that the genetic, archaeological, and earthwork evidence (the Solutrean hypothesis, the Amazonian dark earths, the North American mounds, the Bolivian geoglyphs) is consistent with the presence of a substantial pre-Younger-Dryas civilization in the Americas, with the post-impact archaeological record preserving fragmentary memory of that civilization. The work is the principal recent extension of the catastrophist tradition to the New World and engages directly with the genetic findings of Skoglund, Reich, and others on early American population history.
